title: Mustardy Fluffy Energizing Korean Dried-Shrimp-Goong-Haeng Mandu
description: A flavorful and energizing Korean mandu dish featuring dried shrimp and mustard flavors. Great for a quick breakfast or snack.
instructions: Mortar and pestle pound the mustard seed until slightly crushed.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the rice flour, water, salt, black pepper, and Korean chili flakes gochugaru.
Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small balls, about the size of a small egg.
Place a small ball of dough in the center of a mandu wrapper, followed by a few pieces of soaked and drained dried shrimp goong haeng and some vegan kimchi.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edge of the mandu wrapper.
Fold the wrapper into a triangle, pressing the edges together to seal the mandu.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Heat about 1-2 inches of sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the mandu to the skillet and cook until they are golden brown and crispy on the bottom.
Flip the mandu over and cook for another minute or until they are crispy on the other side.
Serve the mandu hot with a dipping sauce made from a mixture of soy sauce, rice vinegar, and Gochujang.
